macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above 200-Day Moving Average, Signal Last Seen Before 2020 Rally
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $80,275, down 0.62% in 24 hours, after breaking above its 200-day moving average amid market fluctuations and institutional adoption, though analysts caution it's not a guaranteed rally.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $81K Despite Higher-Than-Expected US Inflation Data
items.summary: Bitcoin stayed resilient above $81,000 at $81,117 despite US inflation surpassing forecasts, with altcoins mixed—BNB and TRON up, XRP and Solana down—and global crypto market cap at $2.7 trillion.

items.title: Charles Schwab Launches $12 Trillion Crypto Trading Service for Bitcoin and Ether
items.summary: Charles Schwab began rolling out direct spot trading of Bitcoin and Ether to eligible retail clients across most US states, marking a major Wall Street shift with its $12 trillion in assets.

items.title: CHIP Token Surges 122.54% in Dramatic Rally to $0.09437
items.summary: USD AI (CHIP) token skyrocketed 122.54% on March 21, 2025, with 850% volume increase, driven by liquidity in a volatile market move.

items.title: Cosmos ATOM Jumps Over 9% But Faces Potential Bull Trap Amid Seller Dominance
items.summary: ATOM rallied 9.5% with $250M volume surge, but rising short positions, $374K spot outflows, and liquidation clusters indicate sellers control and risk of trapping buyers.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI CEOs Predict Shift to Agents and AGI by 2026
items.summary: Sam Altman, Jensen Huang, Sundar Pichai, Satya Nadella, and Elon Musk emphasize AI systems, infrastructure, and agents over models, with Elon Musk stating xAI could reach AGI in 2026 and announcing 'macro hard' AI agent system with Tesla.

items.title: CI/CD Evolution for Autonomous AI Agents
items.summary: Traditional CI/CD pipelines are obsolete for thousands of autonomous agents; new approaches use intent-based agent loops, premerge human review of outcomes, and parallel multiverse commits for continuous compute.

items.title: AI Breakthroughs in Antibiotic Discovery
items.summary: Scientists leverage AI to discover new antibiotics, chemical delivery methods into bacteria, and smarter prescribing to combat antimicrobial resistance and prevent an 'antibiotic apocalypse'.

items.title: Local AI Achieves 45-70 Tokens per Second on Consumer Hardware
items.summary: Local AI setups with Ollama deliver 20-35 tokens/sec on current hardware, with predictions that by 2027, it could run 600B parameter models locally, matching cloud capabilities.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: PlayStation Plus May 2026 Games Revealed
items.summary: PlayStation Plus subscribers can now claim EA Sports FC 26, Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, and Nine Sols as the May 2026 monthly games. This is being highlighted as one of the best months for PlayStation Plus offerings this year.

items.title: SCUF Omega PS5 Controller Officially Launches
items.summary: The brand new officially licensed SCUF Omega controller for PS5 has just dropped, featuring performance-focused design and features.

items.title: 007 First Light Confirmed for 60FPS on PS5 Pro
items.summary: IO Interactive released 60FPS footage of 007 First Light running on PS5 Pro, responding to previous criticism. The game is set to release on May 27, 2026.

items.title: Pearl Abyss Confirms DokeV as Next Major Focus
items.summary: Pearl Abyss has provided an update on its post-Crimson Desert projects, confirming DokeV is still in active development and will be the next major focus, while PLAN 8 remains in development but further away.

items.title: Jagex Releases New Gem Bag for RuneScape
items.summary: Jagex has dropped a new Gem Bag feature for RuneScape, with players discussing investment opportunities and in-game economy impacts.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Cisco reports Q3 revenue up 12% YoY to $15.84B, beats estimates, raises FY 2026 forecast, cuts ~4,000 jobs; shares jump 19% after hours
items.summary: Cisco's networking revenue grew 12% year-over-year to $15.84 billion, surpassing $15.56 billion estimates, with raised AI orders forecast despite job cuts; stock surged over 19% in after-hours trading.

items.title: AWS suffers US-East-1 outage after data center thermal event
items.summary: A cooling system failure in AWS's core US-East-1 region caused server overheating and power-downs, impairing EC2 and EBS services and highlighting global reliance on the region.

items.title: eBay rejects GameStop's $56B unsolicited offer as 'neither credible nor attractive'
items.summary: eBay dismissed GameStop's $56 billion bid, stating it lacked credibility and appeal amid ongoing acquisition talks.

items.title: Sony launches Xperia 1 VIII with bigger camera sensors and new design
items.summary: Sony's latest flagship Xperia 1 VIII features larger camera sensors, a redesigned look, and an outrageous price, though not available in the US.

items.title: Samsung launches One UI 9 beta for Galaxy S26 with Android 17 in six markets
items.summary: The first One UI 9 beta based on Android 17 is now available for Galaxy S26 series in select markets, bringing new software features.

items.title: PCI group starts work on PCI Express 8.0 spec for bandwidth-hungry apps like HPC
items.summary: The PCI-SIG began developing PCI Express 8.0, due in 2028, to support high-bandwidth needs in high-speed networking, edge computing, and more.

items.title: LinkedIn lays off 5% of workforce across marketing, engineering, and product teams
items.summary: LinkedIn is cutting 5% of its staff according to a leaked memo, affecting multiple teams amid ongoing tech sector adjustments.
